Pallet final checks: good
Oil is now circulating in melting mode.

When I called Maintenance to help us install the rollers on Chicane belts, he noticed that we were missing one roller —since the third shift was the one that performed the disassembly. We have already searched for it, but we cannot find it.
If the chiller is turned off, we lose pressure in the PTL water. This will fault the heating loop and it will stop heating and can freeze everything from ptl tank to the sweco pipe.

Leave the chiller on. We run glycol in L3 and will not have water freezing issues, the PTL is using the same glycol loop, also different in L3. The chocolate will freeze in the PTL and the strainer filter if we don't leave it on.

Chad Stone Robert Villarreal Rich Brierley Preston Elmer During the cleaning of the buffer tank walls with the pump sprayer. The Brass cap/Fitting fell into the buffer tank. After consulting Robert he advised to start the process over and find the brass Fittings. I have the staff using a mesh screen over each buck to help find the Fitting.
Noze plate. Done
Pass ATP ✅
Mold pass ATP

The tank will need to be drained to remove the nozzle tip before it gets into the pump or temper unit. 
Because it's metal, it will absolutely destroy anything it encounters in the temper unit. Best hope is it gets stuck in the pump and destroys that instead of the temper unit. 
For this reason I ask it be drained and stop pumping oil through the system. 
 
If the metal tip is found while emptying the tank out then circulation can continue as long as all of the tip is found intact
The missing brass fitting was found intact.
PTL done ✅
The missing brass fitting was found intact.
Grey postes the pictures, but the internet is running slow. The fitting was found intact
All the parts in the hopper area are clean and plastic wrapped, because those parts are not the ones we use for the Almond product; only the hopper itself is what we need to run Almond.
When we started reassembly  the Chicane belt 2 we realized that 4 bolts were missing.
Does anyone know where the z conveyor is being stored?
Haha thanks!
Do I need to update the Su checklist to 53?
We need to stop cleaning the cooler momentarily to drain the oil. They’re on the same line and while cleaning we had it LOTO
Ah yes please 🙏 that ended up keeping the temp up
Pipe frozen maintenance is working on getting it unclogged

Robert Villarreal just want to make sure we’re doing 6hrs on the 2nd one? I think last time we just circulated 4hrs for the 2nd one and then on the 3rd we did it until it reached a certain temperature
We finally got it unclogged (thanks Robert Villarreal for the suggestion) it’s low enough now that we’re draining it from the bottom
Yes 6 hours on 2nd, we were trying to speed up the process last time due to visitors coming on site the next day and getting tests back
By Cavanna 2
I checked the four plugs all are correct. There was one ( plug 2) that was upside down. I corrected it though
The belt was started when the heater was up, it knocked it down and there is noticeable damage on the north east side. Maintenance is aware of it.
Does the heater still work Nathan Gevenois, Raoul Lubain?
Jose is checking
The fan is turn on, but maintenance not about the heater element, because of the flushing process they can't turn the imperial.
Maintenance is changing over the cavanna
Maintenance is changing over the cavanna
The unit is leaking oil on the bottom maintenance is checking it
We are starting the process to empty the oil from the buffer tank
FYI I mentioned this May 13th. Tagging everyone but didn't put it in the safety chat so it was passed over. Thank you Raoul Lubain
Started filling the tank

Update: Maintenance is working on Cavanna 1, and the Bosch operator is checking the Bosch machine. QA asked us to continue cleaning the buffer walls a little more with the oil sprayer. We are locating the hopper parts in the warehouse to install them.
Both Cavanna and Bosch techs are here working on their FWs

Depositor water pipe is bent and a little damaged. 
Maintenance has been called to come check on it
Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer Itzel Chavez Frederick Peairs Grey Boscan Nathan Gevenois Raoul Lubain Kimberly Morales Tyson Fausett
It's a lot damaged now. I am at a loss how we can have the standard dosing block in both buildings for years and they are still fine and this one os already broken. The parts are identical.
Good news is the heater is on and survived the crash. It just looks sad now 😞
Cavanna 1 lot is correct

Cavanna is setup and ready to go with the former that we ordered but couldn't use last time. 
Bosch is still being worked on the by the tech - he's just trying to dial in one last flywheel that pops out the film once in a while for some reason.
Quality standards on the line

The rice hose was found installed, when the hose for the almond product should have been installed.





The hose has already been switched from rice to almonds.
The pump block and dousing heads have been installed.

Operators are installing the hopper. The PTL is also being installed to complete the flush, and at 10:15 we will empty the oil from the buffer tank. The technician continues working on the Bosch machine.
Hopper has been installed
We already filled the PTL with 300 lb of oil, and we are now emptying the buffer tank. QA is here during the process.
We are draining the buffer tank, but the oil is not coming out under pressure; Maintenance is already working on it.
While installing the nozzle plate, the operator realized that two parts are missing. We do not know the names of the parts, but they are the ones that hold the blades.
We are filling the caddy with oil now and moving onto the temper unit wash.
First wash is in process
2nd flush started
3rd flush in progress. Oil in the hoses is looking pretty good.
Clean oil is now coming out of the caddy from the temper unit
Currently flushing oil from the buffer to the sweco and into a barrel. Oil is looking a bit dark.
The oil is clean right now
QA has approved the flush from the buffer to the sweco. We are now swapping to the branch pump. Once the branch pump is passed we can begin to push chocolate.
Shadow board at off the 3rd shift molding 3 , the pick is missing
The branch pump flush has been approved . The oil is now clean
We are sending chocolate to the buffer so we can start the push.
Chocolate from the return line looks good QA has approved. Pushing chocolate to branch pump now.
Chocolate from branch pump looks good, QA has approved. We will begin to temper and get things ready to start the line.

Having piston faults while trying to purge the hopper. 

We are pulling the hopper to check everything is installed correctly
Chocolate adjustment report Alan Todd Brandon Lance
Rotary slider A was completely seized up. Maintenance helped get it out of the depositor. We are washing both sliders and cleaning inside where the sliders go into place.
Rotary sliders are in place. Tested  pump system and we have no more piston faults. We are filling the hopper with chocolate so we can start depositing.
When trying to purge chocolate we are getting the same piston faults as before. We are checking sliders again.
We are pulling out the depositor and getting it washed.
While inspecting the S2 clean, QA identified the following areas that require re-cleaning.
Please post pictures further away as well as it is difficult to tell which areas these are
These are the areas. They have all been re-cleaned. Waiting for QA to swab.

Victor in this pic from the HMI the C1, C2, & C3 are part of the same QV area "Jar Filling Conveyor" & C4A is the "Jar filling discharge" which is in section 3.  Thanks for bringing it up for clarification.

QA found this Trio scrap from the T3 washing machine. It is currently being washed.
Mary Montilla, Miriah Manzanares

That is important information.  I am glad we didn't try and tackle that one last night.  
 
Thank you for the detail Chris Jeffs!
Hi Mariel. Good callout. can you please explain to me what Trio scrap is and how this affects the current cleaning on the line?
We need to wash the washing equipment for Tunnel 3.Mariela is referring to Tree Nuts.
is an accumulation of Tree Nuts residue. A Level 4 cleaning is currently being performed, and ATP and allergen tests will be conducted. The line is not running
I see. Thank you for the clarification. I will respond for plan in our QA chat, then we can place final deciions in this chat.
For this issues, since the cleaning is still ongoing, the plan is to reclean this area with the same level 4 criteria as rest of line. no additional testing needed since line has not been released.

We have caramel at the cutter, but we are having issues with Ultrasonic. Caramel is sticking to it. Maintenance on their way to look at it. Everything in the back passed and we are finishing by applying sanitizer Justin Garner

Chad Stone Curt Willis
The nut side has never been opened. We would have to drain the glycol in the system to achieve this goal. We modified the wheat side to be able to remove the end caps without draining glycol.
The motor in Tunnel 3 was washed and passed ATP (17) and allergen pass
Maintenance is doing a hard reset on the blades. They faulted out Justin Garner
Line is running

Laura Brady - I've asked the team to go ahead and label these using the labels that the system provides. I expect the problem is just a type and we should still fix it, but it feels like a better idea to have them labeled now and then decide if we want to over-label later. Let me know if you feel differently.
We have never had anyone complain.  However, I can pout this on my list to correct later when people are out of the labelview file.  We would have to shut this down at all three buildings to fix.

Yeah, that's a much bigger fix. I think we're making the right call though. Candon Watt - is there anything we need to do (beyond documenting in safetychain) to be prepared in case a complaint does come back?
Nope that’s it
L3 closed out of the file and I was able to make the change to the label.  Anything they print from here on will be correct.
Thanks now it’s correct
we finished the WIP 21062 UTT Dark
the second shift will start the changeover to CPK
Pre op done. Lot and BB have been verified. No previous product was found
Pre op done. Lot and BB have been verified. No previous lot was found.
Pallet verification
Startup checklist
The line started at time
We are having issues with the weight. Maintenance is on the line

By checking on the SPEC and the weight card, I observe that the minimum weight does not coincide with the indications required by the SPEC, Having an inconsistency of 10 grams more due to the ideal minimum weight required by the specifications, the leader has already made the relevant adjustments to improve the problem presented by the Checkweigher machine.

Here is the adjustment made to correct the machine, carried out in accordance with the specifications required by the spec; this has effectively resolved the issue we were experiencing with the checkweigher, which had been rejecting all underweight parts.

Check packaging update 


We are within the specification range, 33 pieces per pouch.
Curt Willis Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth is there operators available to help start up the tunnels and be around for what we need please Jorge Carrillo Matt Hatch

The chiller was fixed and the cooling tunnel is cold.

Kitchen 4 has started to cook 1 batch of classic caramel.

Around 1:30, we will start tearing the line by depositing  caramel on the Conbar and sending it down the cooking tunnels.
We have started to deposit caramel on the conveyor belt.
Caramel had a jam at the slitter and one of the separating belts was damaged.
Guillotine needs to be moved west a couple of inches
Slitter was properly set up. Caramel is going through the guillotine, Jacob is making adjustments
Line down Jacob is adjusting the hight and moving the guillotine slightly to the west

Dakota Adair are you going to have available kitchen and guillotine operators that can help through the testing?
Curt Willis

We have less than 20% of the first caramel batch left.

Jakob has requested a 2nd batch of caramel to continue testing the line.

Marcos is starting to make the it and will be ready in approximately 1 hour.

Curt Willis, Erandy Garcilazo Ceja, Justin Garner, & Dakota Adair

S4 test update & plan for tomorrow.
☝️Hi Team. First test of the caramel on the Twist line. The caramel on the conbar  looks very good.
The caramel looks good at the cutting stage.

In this area, we need the pieces that help the candy fall higher on the conveyor belt. Angelica and I are looking at this; perhaps we can shape the edges of the candy from the beginning of the line. 
In this area, we need the pieces that help the candy fall higher on the conveyor belt. Angelica and I are looking at this; perhaps we can shape the edges of the candy from the beginning of the line.
We do not want that piece there anymore, please
Pieces are slightly shorter, we will continue to work on this tomorrow as we just ran out of caramel in the kitchen

TG tech is doing adjustments to chicane belts system but we were able to run some pieces into the wrapper, so far looking good.
Curt Willis

Because we are still short on hight and width some of the caramel pieces fly out of the grippers. 
This will be main thing to address tomorrow

We are going to run classic caramel today to keep testing the front and back of the line. 

When the techs are ready we can start cooking and sending caramel.
Hi Team. Volker is here now

Curt Willis Justin Garner Jorge Carrillo  we will be ready by 9am to to start sending caramel down the line. 
 
Will you send production workers over here to help and who ever needs training please at that time.
They should already be on the line. Operators are in the back with Theegarten
After speaking with the German technicians from each area, they would like us to start cooking at 9:00 a.m.
I think I saw 2 people over there is there anyone to help with the tunnel? Where the cutter is?
Yes, I will send Jesus over shortly to help with cutter
Hi Italu Martinez. I only have one recipe (Bequet). Could you please send me a recipe with the percentage distribution of each ingredient for 500 lb and 1,000 lb batch sizes? Thank you.

Hi Team. Volker wrote to me from the Chicago airport. He said the flight to Salt Lake City was delayed by 3:30, so he will arrive later at Lindon 1.

The caramel yesterday turned out well. We only need to adjust the hopper level in the Conbar program. If you want to continue testing the Twist line, I can cook. Please let me know.
Curt Willis Justin Garner Jorge Carrillo Jorel Gourdin

Curt Willis Jorge Carrillo
Just reach out to Italu Martinez she will be working on the recipe for Bequet caramel batches of 600lb and 700lb, send it as soon as she got it figure it out.

In case that you want her to came over to inspect the caramel, she requested to you guys to reach out, as she needs to plan ahead.

Curt Willis & Italu Martinez, Volker is recommending we cook batches at 1000 lbs since he says they will cook better in K4’s bigger cookers.

When you have a minute, please come to the line to talk with him.
I’ll come out after the 9:45
1 batch has finished cooking and we will now start cooking the 2nd batch.

Pump being reinstalled and tested now

If everything runs well after the pump install, we have approx 4 hours of cookies left
Is an oil flush required for going from white paste to regular milk chocolate?
You could pump oil from the PTL as well
Why would you like to push milk chocolate before flush with oil?
And save the chocolate
Curt Willis recommends it and with the issues we have had, we want to make sure we get it all out of the system.
Will the oil pump from the PTL to the sweco? We’ve never done that before Erandy Garcilazo Ceja
We did it in L3 on the first flushes from the line , we did it in 2 PTLs
